\subsection{Lua}
\label{products-lua}

\begin{tdocnote}
Initially, the {\thisproj} project was created to provide ready-to-use color palettes for {\LUADRAW}, a package that greatly facilitates the creation of high-quality 2D and 3D plots using {\LuaLaTeX} and {\TIKZ}.
\end{tdocnote}

\subsubsection{Basic use}

The {\lua} palette variables are named using the prefix \tdoccodein{lua}+pal+. They are arrays of arrays of three floats (making it straightforward to use a color from a palette). For instance, the following snippet is an excerpt from \tdoccodein{text}+palettes-hf/Accent.lua+.

\begin{tdoccode}{lua}
palAccent = {
    {0.4980392157, 0.7882352941, 0.4980392157},
    {0.7450980392, 0.6823529412, 0.831372549},
    ...
}
\end{tdoccode}

\subsubsection{Creating palettes from existing ones}

The \tdoccodein{lua}+palCreateFromPal+ function provides options to build new palettes by transforming existing ones. The following example shows how to do this (all options are used).

\begin{tdoccode}{lua}
palAccentTransformed = palCreateFromPal(
    palAccent,
    {
        extract = {2, 5, 8, 9},
        shift   = 1,
        reverse = true
    }
)
\end{tdoccode}

\end{document}
